statement.p: If you add {{\frac{10}{3}}} to a certain positive number, then also subtract 1 from that same number, and multiply the results, you get {-{\frac{22}{9}}}\text{.} What was the original number?

solution.p: Let x be the mystery number. We are told that \left(x+{{\frac{10}{3}}}\right)\left(x-1\right)={-{\frac{22}{9}}}\text{.} So
Now we can apply the quadratic formula:
Both {-{\frac{8}{3}}} and {{\frac{1}{3}}} solve the equation, but we were asked to find a positive number. So the mystery number is {{\frac{1}{3}}}\text{.}
